reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): This finding requires a compromised or buggy local Execution Layer client. The code at block_processor.nim:896 intentionally discards entry.columns and uses entry.blobs instead. The data flow is: (1) EL provides blobs via JWT-authenticated Engine API (SEMI_TRUSTED source per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json trust_assumptions), (2) CL converts blobs to columns locally for distributed publishing, (3) CL imports block using blobs only. Malformed columns can only result from malformed blobs from the local EL. The trust_assumptions explicitly state: 'If the EL is compromised or buggy, corrupted data may be propagated to the network.' This is a conditional vulnerability requiring a compromised trusted component, outside the security boundary. The 01e property (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:733-749) states the requirement, but the attack path requires local EL compromise, which is semi-trusted per the bug bounty scope.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: After distributed blob publishing, imported columns must behave as if received via gossip. (assertion: forall col from EL: imported(col) => treated_as_gossip(col) == true)
